Name: George Washington
Book mini-review: Fantastic. Deeply researched, engaging, readable, well paced.

Name: John Adams
Book mini-review: Does its very best with a dull as dishwater subject. Detailed and well-written but a slog.

Name: Thomas Jefferson
Book: Thomas Jefferson: The Art of Power
Book mini-review: Brilliantly written, full of great detail, pulls its punches on some of his darker aspects but doesn't ignore them.

Name: James Madison
Book: The Three Lives of James Madison: Genius, Partisan, President
Book mini-review: Detailed and engaging, a little longer than its subject deserves.

Name: James Monroe
Book: The Last Founding Father: James Monroe and a Nation's Call to Greatness
Book mini-review: Concise, about as long as this shitty dude deserved, but too nice to him.

Name: John Quincy Adams
Book mini-review: JQA was even duller than his dad. The bio is workmanlike with much less of the color of other bios.

Name: Andrew Jackson
Book: Andrew Jackson: His Life and Times
Book mini-review: Military-focused, containing less personal detail than is probably warranted, but very readable.

Name: Martin Van Buren
Book: Martin Van Buren: A Captivating Guide to the Man Who Served as the Eighth President of the United States
Book mini-review: Short and cliff notes-y but with some fun details, and very engaging. Best of the cliff-notes bios.

Name: William Henry Harrison
Book: William Henry Harrison: The Life and Legacy of the First American President to Die in Office
Book mini-review: Just under 2 hours long, a cliff-notes bio. Not great.

Name: John Tyler
Book mini-review: 28 minutes long, a shitty all-the-presidents entry.

Name: James K Polk
Book: Polk: The Man Who Transformed the Presidency and America
Book mini-review: Author wanted to write about Jackson and sort of accidentally wrote about Polk. Not good.

Name: Zachary Taylor
Book mini-review: A 24-minute all-the-presidents entry. Taylor sucked but so does this bio.

Name: Millard Fillmore
Book mini-review: 17 minutes long! The shortest bio yet, a worthless all-the-presidents entry.

Name: Franklin Pierce
Book mini-review: Another all-the-presidents entry. A few fun tidbits.

Name: James Buchanan
Book: Worst. President. Ever. James Buchanan, the POTUS Rating Game, and the Legacy of the Least of the Lesser Presidents
Book mini-review: A delightful treatment of a shitty subject, light-hearted but well researched, very engaging.

Name: Abraham Lincoln
Book: Team of Rivals: The Political Genius of Abraham Lincoln
Book mini-review: DKG has a well-earned reputation as an excellent biographer and Lincoln is an amazing subject. 42 hours of material fly by.

Name: Andrew Johnson
Book: After Lincoln: How the North Won the Civil War and Lost the Peace
Book mini-review: Not really a bio of Johnson, but covers the aftermath of Lincoln so mostly about Johnson. Good but little personal detail about Johnson for that reason.

Name: Ulysses S Grant
Book: American Ulysses: A Life of Ulysses S. Grant
Book mini-review: Fantastic book of a truly great and under-rated president. Great details.

Name: Rutherford B Hayes
Book mini-review: 5 hours long which is 4 hours more than its subject deserved. Yawnfest.

Name: James A Garfield
Book: James A Garfield: The 20th President's 200 Days in Office
Book mini-review: An hour long but left me wanting much more.

Name: Chester A Arthur
Book: The Unexpected President: The Life and Times of Chester A. Arthur
Book mini-review: Recommended only for the excellent details of Julia Sand's letters.

Name: Grover Cleveland
Book: An Honest President: The Life and Presidencies of Grover Cleveland
Book mini-review: A mediocre bio of a mediocre guy.

Name: Benjamin Harrison
Book mini-review: An all-the-presidents series entry, just 22 minutes long, barely worth it.

Name: William McKinley
Book mini-review: An all-the-presidents entry much longer than it needed to be.

Name: Theodore Roosevelt
Book mini-review: The first of an epic 3-book series on TR of which I read 2. Definitive and captivating.

Name: William Howard Taft
Book: William Howard Taft: The American Presidents Series: The 27th President, 1909-1913
Book Author: Jeffrey Rosen, Arthur M. Schlesinger Jr., Sean Wilentz
Book mini-review: Lacks compelling personal details that could have fit in 5 hours. Not compelling.

Name: Woodrow Wilson
Book mini-review: Way too long given how dull Wilson is but well-written.

Name: Warren G Harding
Book mini-review: Short and dull but still not as bad as The Bloviator, which is awful and nobody should read.

Name: Calvin Coolidge
Book mini-review: A concise and insightful look at an important transitional president.

Name: Herbert Hoover
Book: Hoover: An Extraordinary Life in Extraordinary Times
Book mini-review: I could have read another 50% of this 27-hour book. Fascinating.

Name: Franklin D Roosevelt
Book mini-review: A gem of a book, full of amazing details, and a wonderful subject.

Name: Harry S Truman
Book mini-review: Well-written and detailed but insufficiently critical of its surprisingly crooked subject.

Name: Dwight D Eisenhower
Book mini-review: Sort of a side-effect of the FDR bio but well-written and workable.

Name: John F Kennedy
Book: An Unfinished Life: John F Kennedy, 1917-1963
Book mini-review: A good book but cruelly, pointlessly abridged, please release the full thing.

Name: Lyndon B Johnson
Book: Lyndon Johnson and the American Dream: The Most Revealing Portrait of a President and Presidential Power Ever Written
Book mini-review: A mind-blowing book of amazing depth and quality of writing from an author incredibly close to her subject. Unmissable.

Name: Richard Nixon
Book mini-review: Detailed and pulls few punches, full of great personal details.

Name: Gerald Ford
Book: Ambition, Pragmatism, and Party: A Political Biography of Gerald R. Ford
Book mini-review: Ford truly sucked, and this comprehensive but mercifully concise book covers it well.

Name: Jimmy Carter
Book: President Carter: The White House Years
Book mini-review: This bio is WAY TOO LONG for the amount of interest; a book half the length would be fine.

Name: Ronald Reagan
Book mini-review: Solid and comprehensive; a better book will be written when more stuff is declassified.

Name: George H W Bush
Book: Destiny and Power: The American Odyssey of George Herbert Walker Bush
Book mini-review: Comprehensive, hagiographic, far too long.
